tools.name: OCR
tools.description: This tool can recognize all text on the input image.

outputs.description: OCR results, include bbox in x1, y1, x2, y2 format and the recognized text.

tools.name: ImageDescription
tools.description: A useful tool that returns a brief description of the input image.

tools.name: RegionAttributeDescription
tools.description: Describe the attribute of a region of the input image.

inputs.name: bbox
inputs.description: The bbox coordinate in the format of `(x1, y1, x2, y2)`

dialogs.content: You are given a CT image, which part is the scan of?

dialogs.thought: Check for laterality markers and scan parameters to orient the axial CT image before localizing the abnormality.
Get an overall description of the CT slice (anatomy level and any obvious abnormal tract/hernia) to guide targeted regional inspection.

content.content: Axial contrast-enhanced CT image through the lower abdomen/pelvis at the level of the groins. Pelvic bones and proximal femora are seen with soft tissues of the inguinal regions bilaterally; there is asymmetry in the left inguinal canal region suggesting a patent processus vaginalis/inguinal communication.

arguments.attribute: inguinal canal/groin region appearance and any tubular fluid/soft-tissue tract suggesting a patent processus vaginalis

dialogs.thought: Interrogate the left groin/inguinal canal region for a communicating tract consistent with patent processus vaginalis.

content.content: In the left inguinal region there is a small tubular/linear soft-tissue density extending along the expected course of the inguinal canal with a subtle communication toward the peritoneal cavity, compatible with a patent processus vaginalis rather than a discrete mass.

dialogs.content: The main finding in the computed tomography (CT) scan of the abdomen and pelvis is a patent processus vaginalis on the left side.
